deep_dive.title: Beyond Agent Architecture: Execution Assumptions and Reproducibility in LLM-Based Trading Systems
deep_dive.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) and agentic systems are increasingly proposed for financial trading, yet their reported performance remains difficult to compare because studies vary in data provenance, temporal split discipline, execution timing, turnover treatment, and transaction-cost modeling. This article presents a targeted topical review and reproducibility audit of execution realism in LLM-based trading research. A coded evidence matrix covering 30 trade-relevant primary studies is used to assess point-in-time controls, split transparency, held-out evaluation, cost and turnover treatment, execution semantics, universe definition, and artifact release. Across the audited sample, architecture reporting is generally clearer than the evaluation assumptions needed to judge whether a trading result is economically interpretable or reproducible. A 10-equity worked example is included only as a methodological scaffold to illustrate how explicit friction and timing choices can materially compress active-strategy results. The main conclusion is that the next useful step for LLM trading research is not only better agent design, but also clearer reporting standards for execution realism, reproducibility, and evaluation comparability.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Review of LLM-based trading agents, focusing on execution realism and reproducibility
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 回顾基于LLM的金融交易智能体，关注执行真实性和可复现性
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 回顾基于LLM的金融交易智能体，关注执行真实性和可复现性
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 回顾基于LLM的金融交易智能体，关注执行真实性和可复现性
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: The paper presents a topical review and reproducibility audit of execution assumptions in LLM-based trading systems, highlighting inconsistencies across 30 primary studies.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文对基于LLM的金融交易系统进行了专题回顾与可复现性审计，重点检查执行假设、数据划分和交易成本等关键环节。通过对30项研究编码证据矩阵，发现虽架构报告趋透明，但执行语义和成本建模等细节常被忽视，导致结果难比较。该工作揭示了LLM交易智能体研究的可复现性危机，为建立严格评估标准提供依据。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文对基于LLM的金融交易系统进行了专题回顾与可复现性审计，重点检查执行假设、数据划分和交易成本等关键环节。通过对30项研究编码证据矩阵，发现虽架构报告趋透明，但执行语义和成本建模等细节常被忽视，导致结果难比较。该工作揭示了LLM交易智能体研究的可复现性危机，为建立严格评估标准提供依据。

deep_dive.title: SAGE: An LLM-driven Self Reflective Agentic Framework for Fraud Detection
deep_dive.abstract: Fraud detection in payment, e-commerce, and telecommunications systems requires accuracy at the individual level, robustness under severe class imbalance, and ease of understanding for risk managers. Existing methods fall at least one of these requirements: automated machine learning systems search a fixed numerical space without semantic awareness of the dataset; graph neural network-based methods require pre-defined relational graphs and remain opaque at the individual-decision level; and the design of general-purpose large language model (LLM) agents does not consider the recall and precision constraints specific to real-world fraud detection. In this paper, we propose SAGE, the first end-to-end LLM-driven multi-agent framework for fraud detection. SAGE coordinates three dedicated agents that make decisions based on a six-layer Data Diagnostic Tree (DDT) and a Markov decision process guided by natural-language gradients, automatically optimizing the model under a fraud-specific reward. On five fraud datasets and five LLM backbones, SAGE wins $96.00\%$ of method--dataset comparisons and improves F1 by an average of $40.86\%$ over baselines. The code is available at https://github.com/yichenC1c/SAGE.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: LLM-driven multi-agent framework for fraud detection in finance
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: LLM驱动的金融欺诈检测多智能体框架
deep_dive.llm_evidence: LLM驱动的金融欺诈检测多智能体框架
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: LLM驱动的金融欺诈检测多智能体框架
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: SAGE presents an end-to-end LLM-driven multi-agent framework for financial fraud detection, addressing precision and recall constraints.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对金融欺诈检测中个体级准确率、极端类别不平衡与可解释性挑战，SAGE提出首个端到端LLM多智能体框架，通过自反思机制提升欺诈识别效果，为金融领域任务型智能体构建提供了范例。

deep_dive.title: Knowing When to Ask: Self-Gated Clarification for Hierarchical Language Agents
deep_dive.abstract: In hierarchical reasoning, failures often originate at intermediate decision points where the agent commits to a wrong branch without recognizing that it lacks critical information. Rather than treating clarification as an external uncertainty trigger, we propose ACTION-RATING, a formulation that places it inside the agent's action space on a shared ordinal scale with navigation, so that asking competes directly with acting at every decision point and help-seeking becomes observable at intermediate states. Two structurally distinct information-seeking modes emerge from the agent's own ratings: mandatory (no viable branch) and opportunistic (residual uncertainty despite a leading candidate). On Harmonized Tariff Schedule classification (30,000-node taxonomy, three benchmarks, 9~LLMs across 4 families), we observe a regime shift from mandatory to opportunistic clarification, with Information-Seeking Effectiveness (ISE), a local diagnostic defined as the fraction of help interactions followed by a correct next navigation step (not a final-task metric), rising from 50% to 74%. Three diagnostic contrasts fail to reproduce this structure. A separability test shows that the information-seeking pattern (mode split, ISE ranking) persists when answer quality is degraded (-18.8% accuracy), supporting an empirical separation between where an agent seeks help and the quality of the help it receives. Under the controlled answer channel, accuracy gains reach +16.2% at 10-digit; we read this as an upper bound on what better localization could unlock, not a deployment estimate.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: applies self-gated clarification to agents performing Harmonized Tariff Schedule classification, a financial domain task
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 将自门控澄清应用于海关税则分类智能体，属于金融领域任务导向智能体
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 将自门控澄清应用于海关税则分类智能体，属于金融领域任务导向智能体
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 将自门控澄清应用于海关税则分类智能体，属于金融领域任务导向智能体
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: The paper proposes ACTION-RATING for hierarchical language agents, allowing them to decide when to ask for clarification, and demonstrates it on the financial task of Harmonized Tariff Schedule classification.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对层次化语言智能体在决策时因信息不足而犯错的问题，提出ACTION-RATING方法，将询问澄清作为与导航同级的动作引入动作空间。在金融领域的海关税则分类任务上，智能体学会了必要时主动求助，减少了盲目分支导致的错误，证明了该方法对于金融任务导向智能体的有效性。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文针对层次化语言智能体在决策时因信息不足而犯错的问题，提出ACTION-RATING方法，将询问澄清作为与导航同级的动作引入动作空间。在金融领域的海关税则分类任务上，智能体学会了必要时主动求助，减少了盲目分支导致的错误，证明了该方法对于金融任务导向智能体的有效性。

quick_skim.title: SPA: A SQL-Plan-Aware Reinforcement Learning Framework for Query Rewriting with LLMs
quick_skim.abstract: SQL query rewriting is a well-established technique for improving database performance without schema or index changes, yet finding effective rewrites for modern analytical workloads remains difficult: rule-based methods are limited to predefined transformations, while LLM-based approaches often produce rewrites that are semantically valid but compile to equivalent physical plans or degrade runtime performance. We present SPA, a SQL-Plan-Aware reinforcement learning framework that trains LLMs to rewrite queries using physical execution feedback. SPA formulates rewriting as a policy optimization problem and extends GRPO with rewards spanning semantic equivalence, textual rewrite distance, physical-plan divergence, and runtime speedup. To handle reward sparsity across query difficulty, SPA introduces Probability-Gated Adaptive Reward Shaping, a query-level curriculum that unlocks higher-level rewards only once a rollout group achieves sufficient mastery of lower-level objectives, and further improves sample efficiency through on-policy self-improvement by recycling slowdown rewrites from the current policy as targeted training signals. On both IID and OOD workloads, SPA outperforms rule-based and strong LLM baselines in end-to-end runtime, substantially reduces harmful slowdown rewrites, and yields strong tail-latency gains.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: RL post-training for LLMs on task-specific policy with GRPO extension
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于强化学习的LLM策略后训练方法
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于强化学习的LLM策略后训练方法
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于强化学习的LLM策略后训练方法
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: SPA proposes a reinforcement learning framework to train LLMs for SQL query rewriting using physical execution feedback and multi-objective rewards, extending GRPO.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: SPA提出SQL-Plan-Aware强化学习框架，利用物理执行反馈训练LLM进行查询重写，解决现有方法生成等价计划或性能下降的问题。该方法将重写建模为策略优化，扩展GRPO并引入语义等价、文本距离、物理计划差异和运行时间加速等奖励。实验表明有效提升查询性能，为LLM任务特定后训练提供了新范式。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: SPA提出SQL-Plan-Aware强化学习框架，利用物理执行反馈训练LLM进行查询重写，解决现有方法生成等价计划或性能下降的问题。该方法将重写建模为策略优化，扩展GRPO并引入语义等价、文本距离、物理计划差异和运行时间加速等奖励。实验表明有效提升查询性能，为LLM任务特定后训练提供了新范式。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: techniques for post-training of autonomous agents

quick_skim.title: Addressing Market Regime Changes and Heavy-Tailed Returns in Portfolio Optimization via Bayesian VAR and Elliptical Black-Litterman
quick_skim.abstract: Deep reinforcement learning (DRL) frameworks for portfolio optimization have shown promise for their ability to learn allocation rules dynamically from market data. However, these models fail to account for fat-tailed returns, which characterize actual market behavior with more frequent extreme events. Furthermore, historical data is treated homogeneously, without accounting for temporal importance, leading models to fail during regime changes. We propose a new BAVAR-BLED algorithm that combines methods derived from Bayesian-Averaging Vector Autoregressive (BAVAR) and the Black-Litterman model using Elliptical Distributions (BLED) within a TD3 architecture. BAVAR captures a set of vector autoregressive representations that consider multi-scale temporal features, enabling adaptive allocation decisions based on regime-aware estimates of return expectations and dispersion matrices. These estimates serve as prior inputs to BLED, a model that uses Student's t-distributions, allowing for more realistic fat tail return estimates. The BAVAR-BLED algorithm uses transformer networks for view construction and CNNs for risk-aversion estimates, which modify dynamic allocation decisions based on market conditions. An evaluation of 29 Dow Jones Industrial Average constituents over a decade-long market period shows that BAVAR-BLED significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods, achieving Sharpe and Sortino ratios of 1.72 and 2.70, respectively, and total returns of 57.26%.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: proposes DRL portfolio optimization agent accounting for fat-tailed returns and regime changes, a financial task-oriented agent
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出考虑厚尾收益和机制转换的DRL投资组合优化智能体，属于金融领域任务型智能体
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出考虑厚尾收益和机制转换的DRL投资组合优化智能体，属于金融领域任务型智能体
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出考虑厚尾收益和机制转换的DRL投资组合优化智能体，属于金融领域任务型智能体
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: The paper proposes a BAVAR-BLED algorithm that combines Bayesian VAR and Elliptical Black-Litterman within a TD3 DRL agent for portfolio optimization, addressing heavy-tailed returns and market regime changes.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对深度强化学习在投资组合优化中忽略厚尾收益和市场机制转换的问题，提出BAVAR-BLED算法，将贝叶斯平均VAR和椭圆分布Black-Litterman模型集成到TD3架构中。实验表明该方法在市场突变时更具稳健性，为金融领域任务型智能体提供了更贴近现实市场的训练方案，但未使用LLM，属于传统DRL。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文针对深度强化学习在投资组合优化中忽略厚尾收益和市场机制转换的问题，提出BAVAR-BLED算法，将贝叶斯平均VAR和椭圆分布Black-Litterman模型集成到TD3架构中。实验表明该方法在市场突变时更具稳健性，为金融领域任务型智能体提供了更贴近现实市场的训练方案，但未使用LLM，属于传统DRL。
